retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Shape and Size -- Differential Diagnosis -- Fusion
retrieved_docs.content: Macrodontia Gemination Talon cusp

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Shape and Size -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: Fusion may pose an aesthetic concern due to the altered shape and size of the affected tooth. These teeth are also at an increased risk of decay and periodontal disease and may complicate endodontic therapy.

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Shape and Size -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Taurodont teeth present challenges across multiple dental specialties. The large pulp chamber and prominent pulp horns increase the risk of pulp exposure due to decay. Variability in pulp chamber size and shape, including apically positioned canal orifices and accessory root canal systems, complicates endodontic therapy. [26]

retrieved_docs.title: Anatomy, Permanent Dentition -- Physiologic Variants
retrieved_docs.content: An additional cusp called the cusp of Carabelli is sometimes located on the mesio-palatal surface of permanent maxillary first molars and is rarely present on the other permanent molars. Although less common, an accessory cusp can protrude from the cingulum of the primary or permanent anterior teeth, referred to as the talon cusp. [26] Macrodontia and microdontia are rare shape anomalies of dentition, where teeth are larger or smaller than average. [27] [28] Taurodontism is an abnormality of tooth morphology that often occurs in multi-rooted teeth and is characterized by a short root and an enlarged body containing an equally enlarged pulp chamber. [29]

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Shape and Size -- History and Physical
retrieved_docs.content: Taurodontism is typically diagnosed radiographically during routine dental visits, as patients are usually unaware of the condition. [22] Fusion presents as an enlarged clinical crown resulting from the union of 2 normally separate tooth germs during development (see Image. Dental Fusion in Primary Dentition).

retrieved_docs.content: Fusion Enamel hypoplasia Gemination

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Shape and Size -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: Macrodontia results from the overexpression of developmental signals, leading to hyperactivity in the tissues involved in tooth formation. This anomaly has been linked to systemic, syndromic, and environmental factors and produces teeth that are larger than normal.

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Shape and Size -- Differential Diagnosis -- Macrodontia

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Shape and Size -- Differential Diagnosis -- Taurodontism
retrieved_docs.content: Amelogenesis imperfecta Dentinogenesis imperfecta X-chromosomal Aneuploidy

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Shape and Size -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: As mentioned, taurodontism presents restorative, prosthodontic, endodontic, and surgical management challenges. Despite these complications, patients are typically unaware of the condition unless dental intervention is required.

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Shape and Size -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: Taurodontism has been attributed to 3 proposed pathological mechanisms: an atypical developmental pattern causing delayed calcification of the pulp chamber, a deficiency of odontoblasts causing alterations in the Hertwig epithelial root sheath, or a disruption in developmental homeostasis. [18]

retrieved_docs.title: Trauma to the Primary Dentition -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: Due to the close relationship between the apex of the primary tooth root and the underlying permanent tooth germ, there is potential for sequelae to the permanent tooth following orofacial and primary tooth trauma.

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Number -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: Hypodontia and oligodontia often fall under the broader classification of “hypodontia,” with specific distinctions made based on the number of missing teeth. Hypodontia represents the most common dentofacial malformation in humans. [7] A clear correlation exists between hypodontia in the deciduous (primary) dentition and the corresponding permanent dentition; patients missing a primary tooth frequently lack its permanent successor. [8]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 243
retrieved_docs.content: Disorders of teeth and jaw are classified under the broader parent category zuby and are specifically associated with the anatomical location of zuby mléčné, the primary (milk) teeth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 249
retrieved_docs.content: Dental procedures are performed directly on zuby mléčné (the primary or “milk” teeth), indicating that therapeutic dental interventions often target the primary dentition. Likewise, disorders of teeth and jaw are located in zuby mléčné, showing that many oral pathologies also affect these early‑life teeth. Together, these relationships emphasize that both clinical treatments and disease processes in pediatric dentistry are closely linked to the primary teeth, underscoring the importance of careful assessment and management of zuby mléčné in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 250
retrieved_docs.content: The extraction of a single tooth is a direct dental procedure performed on the zuby (teeth), involving the removal of one tooth from the oral cavity.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 1 -- Community 226
retrieved_docs.content: Dental procedures are directly performed on zuby mléčné, indicating that primary (baby) teeth serve as the specific sites for these dental interventions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 1 -- Community 225
retrieved_docs.content: Extraction of single tooth is a direct procedure performed on zuby (teeth), indicating that the teeth are the specific anatomical site for this dental intervention. Disorders of teeth and jaw are described as having zuby as their parent category, meaning that conditions affecting the teeth and jaw are organized under the broader classification of zuby. Consequently, zuby functions both as the procedural target for single‑tooth extraction and as the foundational element within the hierarchy of dental and jaw disorders, underscoring its central importance in dental treatment and pathology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 244
retrieved_docs.content: Extraction of single tooth is classified as a dental procedure, indicating that it has a relationship with the broader category of dental procedures. This connection underscores that the act of extracting a single tooth is a specific type of dental intervention within the overall field of dental procedures.

retrieved_docs.content: Cervical shortening in pregnancy is categorized as a component of other complications of birth and puerperium that influence the management of the mother, and it specifically encompasses the sub‑condition of cervical shortening occurring in the second trimester.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 4 -- Community 2732
retrieved_docs.content: Other specified conditions related to pregnancy comprise a range of distinct gestational disorders. This category includes renal disorder during pregnancy, peripheral neuropathy during pregnancy, and polymorphic eruption of pregnancy, each representing a separate organ‑system complication that can arise in the context of gestation and is classified under the broader label of other specified pregnancy‑related conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 4 -- Community 2885
retrieved_docs.content: COMPLICATIONS MAINLY RELATED TO PREGNANCY is classified as a subcategory within the broader group of Complications of pregnancy, childbirth and the puerperium, indicating that these health issues are primarily associated with the gestational period and its immediate aftermath.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 2 -- Community 1057
retrieved_docs.content: grouped under a unified late‑effect classification that is further subdivided by anatomical region. This structured relationship aids clinicians in linking acute injury diagnoses with their potential long‑term sequelae and facilitates comprehensive patient management across different injury types and body regions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 3 -- Community 2732
retrieved_docs.content: Accidental physical contact (event) is described as being due to loss of teeth caused by an accident, indicating that dental injury resulting from an accident can lead to unintended physical contact.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2900
retrieved_docs.content: CONDITIONS OF FETAL GROWTH AND MALNUTRITION represent a collective group of perinatal problems that encompass short gestation, low birth weight, and fetal growth retardation. Short gestation, defined as delivery before full term, often results in low birth weight because the infant has had insufficient time for normal growth. Low birth weight, in turn, frequently reflects fetal growth retardation, a condition in which the fetus does not achieve its genetically expected size. These three entities are closely interrelated, each contributing to the broader spectrum of fetal growth and malnutrition disorders, and together they increase the risk of adverse neonatal outcomes. Effective maternal care that optimizes nutrition and prevents premature delivery is essential for reducing the incidence of these interconnected conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 72
retrieved_docs.content: The category “diseases of mouth; excluding dental” is classified as a subcategory of “اِضطِراب الغدة اللُّعابِيَّة” (salivary gland disorder), indicating that non‑dental oral diseases are considered part of the broader spectrum of salivary gland pathology. This relationship highlights the importance of evaluating salivary gland function when assessing oral conditions that are not of dental origin, thereby guiding more comprehensive diagnosis and patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 31 -- Level 1 -- Community 183
retrieved_docs.content: Complications of pregnancy, childbirth and the puerperium encompass a wide range of obstetric problems, including general maternal conditions, early or threatened labor, hemorrhagic events (abruptio placentae, placenta previa, spotting and unspecified‑trimester hemorrhage), hypertensive disorders (hypertension complicating pregnancy, childbirth and the puerperium and hypertension AND/OR vomiting), abnormal delivery findings (face delivery), partial molar pregnancy, and miscarriage complicated by genital‑pelvic infection. Cervical shortening (short cervical length in pregnancy) and cervical stenosis are classified as obstetric cervical abnormalities and fall under “other maternal conditions that affect the course of gestation.” Cervical shortening is further divided into second‑trimester, third‑trimester and unspecified‑trimester subtypes; the second‑ and third‑trimester forms are specifically linked to early or threatened labor, also described in Arabic as مخاض كاذب (false labor) and

Recognizing these relationships aids clinicians in systematically assessing and managing the long‑term sequelae of diverse traumatic injuries.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1494
retrieved_docs.content: A gestational age of 29‑30 weeks completed is associated with a liveborn infant, indicating that pregnancies reaching this stage can result in a live birth.
